name: Human arterial telomere uncapping increased with age independent of mean telomere length
summary: Human artery data support target relevance for telomere capping, but not a treatment effect.
statement: In skeletal-muscle feed arteries from 104 adults, older age was associated with greater telomere uncapping and p53/p21 senescence signaling, while mean telomere length was not associated with uncapping or senescence.
population_or_model: Human artery samples from younger, middle-aged, and older adults
quantitative_note: The source reports approximately twofold greater p-H2AX localized to telomeres and TRF2 bound to telomeres in older adults versus younger adults.
caveats: This is observational tissue biomarker evidence, not an intervention.
It does not identify a capping-directed therapy or demonstrate functional improvement from restoring shelterin biology.
